rulings.Case Title: 49 ZOE STREET HOMEOWERS'ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHARLES O.JONES CO.,INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 9, 2008, line 3, DEFENDANTS CHARLES O.JONES CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, ZOE ASSOCIATES LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. BY USING THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL FORM WITH ONLY LIMITED ADDITIONS, PLAINTIFFS HAVE FAILED TO SET FORTH THE REQUISITE FACTS NECESSARY TO ESTABLISH THEIR RELATIONSHIP WITH DEFENDANTS AND THE BASIS FOR THEIR CLAIMS AGAINST THE DEFENDANTS. PLAINTIFFS MUST ALLEGE WHEN THE CAUSES OF ACTION AROSE, THE FACTUAL BASIS FOR THE CAUSE OF ACTION AND IF BASED ON A WRITING, SUFFICIENT DETAIL TO ASCERTAIN THE TERMS OF THE WRITING. WITH RESPECT TO THE NEGLIGENCE PER SE CLAIMS, PLAINTIFFS MUST PROVIDE GREATER SPECIFICITY AS TO THE STATUTE(S) OR REGULATION(S) AT ISSUE. PLAINTIFFS ALSO MUST BE CLEAR AS TO HOW DEFENDANTS CONDUCT INJURED PLAINTIFFS. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: 49 ZOE STREET HOMEOWERS'ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHARLES O.JONES CO.,INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 9, 2008, line 3, DEFENDANT CHARLES O.JONES CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, AND ZOE ASSOCIATES LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR BASED ON THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN E LAWRENCE VS. SEAN MCKEAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Attornes Fees And Judgement Against Pltf Stephen E. Lawrence; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 9, 2008, line 10, DEFENDANTS SEAN MCKEAN, SHAWN MCILVENNA'S Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Judgement Against Plaintiff Stephen E. Lawrence. THE MOTION IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH THE PARTIES HAD A LANDLORD - TENANT RELATIONSHIP, THE COMPLAINT FILED DEALT WITH TORTIOUS CONDUCT THAT DID NOT ARISE OUT OF THE EXECUTION OF THE LEASE. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: MARK WILLIAMS VS. JOSE LUIS HERNANDEZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 9, 2008, line 12, DEFENDANT JOSE LUIS HERNANDEZ MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION IS DENIED. IN ROSSETTO V. BARROSS (2001), THE APPELLATE DEPARTMENT OF THE SAN MATEO SUPERIOR COURT LOOKED TO THE LANGUAGE OF THE PARTIES' AGREEMENTS THAT REFERENCED RENT AND DEFENDANT'S EXCLUSIVE RIGHT TO THE PREMISES TO FIND A LANDLORD-TENANT RELATIONSHIP. IN BERRY V. SOCIETY OF SAINT PIUS X (1999) 69 CAL.APP.4TH 354 363, THE COURT OF APPEALS EMPHASIZED THE LIMITED STATUTORY SITUATIONS EMBRACED BY THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION. WITHOUT FURTHER EVIDENCE, IT IS THE COURT'S CONCLUSION THAT A TENANTS' IN COMMON RELATIONSHIP DOES NOT BECOME A LANDLORD-TENANT RELATIONSHIP AS A CONSEQUENCE OF A BREACH OF THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT AND THE PARTIES HAVE CITED NO AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THE PARTIES MAY CONTRACT TO DO SO. =(302/PJM/JW)
